text: Assurance is garbage don’t get it.  They wanted to charge me $100 for replacing g a phone for a broken screen.  I pay eighty a month for insurance on all my phones and then they want to charge me $100.  It only costs $60.00 for a new screen.  Feeling stupid for letting this garbage company take advantage of me.

i visit  store 9933 in christana mall3 in delaware . The staff Antonio, Derrick & Amanda was outstanding . My fiancé was having problems with his phone for several months we visited several stores from Delaware to NYC no one was able to help us with his phone. we walk into to this particular t-mobile & Antonio did the job. he was so good &professional to our needs . not only did he solve the issue . i purchase a new iphone 12. I just want to say the staff at this location is outstanding. 🙏🏾

After hours of calling with customer support to move my subscriptions to my new place and keep my discounts, and after being told It was all in place and working with the discounts... I get an invoice without them... I go to support and they tell me I had to accept some terms that I had miss or something, then the support person proceeds to accept for me and "fixes" the issue, but then tells me that I cannot get back the money... I was under the impression everything was ready, I did not know about any terms I had to accept to get my discount back, they said at first it was all ready... So thanks for nothing T-Mobile...

HORRIBLE CUSTOMER SERVICE..I HATE T MOBILE 
ALWAYS LOOSING INTERNET CONNECTIONS...
TO UPGRADE THEY WANT UPFRONT DEPOSITS INSTEAD OF ADDING IT TO YOUR BILL..NO THANK YOU 
TIME TO CHANGE PROVIDERS

edits.text: Ok..I never chose TMobile..was just slammed into it...Suddenly my unlimited has become, "you're low on data, certain whatever may slow down"..now you're forced charged to make pay arrangements..."if a customer service rep has to input the arrangement you pay for that..on a system that's programmed to where you cannot make the arrangement...blatent trickery

text: TERRIBLE, I HAVE BEEN WITH THEM SINCE 2014, I HAD LTE SPEEDS ALL THE TIME UNTIL THEY MERGE WITH ANOTHER COMPANY, NOW JUST ONE BAR IF IM LUCKY, NO INTERNET AT ALL!!DONT DONT YOU GET T-MOBILE!!!

I’d give it no stars if I could. Constant dropped calls, never have internet service (despite paying for the best), my mother who is also a t-Mobil carrier is NEVER ABLE to call and keep a call going. Bad in store service, and to top it off they are total LIARS. 2nd best network in my state my eye, they are freaking liars fudging the numbers. False advertising.

I have experienced terrible internet service since the merger. Phone calls are poor service now as well. I have been a Sprint customer since 2005 and was very happy with their service. Now I suppose I will have to change carriers and start over. Why didn’t Sprint take over T mobile?  Sprint is so much better. I changed my SIM card, I thought maybe that would help but I think It got worse. My iPhone X has 1 bar most of the time and sometimes it says I am not connected to the internet at all. How can an iPhone not be connected to the internet?

T-Mobile is the worst company I have ever done business with. I have never had worse customer service anywhere ever. Going to cancel all of my lines with Sprint before they go the same route. If you are considering T-Mobile or are a current sprint customer get out while you still can. Going to have to pay more at Verizon but I have been a customer in the past and they always had excellent customer service.

Idiotic store representative at the Gresham, OR store went to put me through for an upgrade from the iphone 8plus to an iphone x.  Couldn't do it, but restarted my installment plan.  Now almost two years later, I am getting collections calls for $304.60 that I don't owe.  Both Chris and Louie on the phone admitted that the IMEI were mismatched and mislabeled on the iphone x when my account is clearly showing that I last used the iphone 8 plus.  But they will NOT waive the fraudulent charges.

After being a loyal customer with AT&T for 21 years decided to switch to T-Mobile. Got new phones but no trade in labels or details. Finally, contacted T-Mobile and was told “you are 1 day delay ”. Despite, wanted to be loyal customer was asked to either pay $2196 and cancel T-Mobile or pay the same amount in monthly installments. Very unfortunate with the service I got from T-Mobile. Only alternative given to pay full and quit, without offering any work around. Very disappointing, unfortunate..unhappy with terrible service from T-Mobile. I was asked to give a feedback and rating on service provided. What should I do?
This company is the worse I switched to Verizon couldn’t stand them anymore

My husband got a new phone from T-Mobile. It was a special promotion so he (1) surrendered his old phone and (2) paid for the new phone up front. Except the kid at the store who sold him the phone signed him up for the wrong plan. So we started getting phantom "lease" charges on our bill. He spent at least 8 hours talking to T-Mobile's customer support number and finally everything seemed to have been fixed. But the next month the "lease" charges continued and even increased. This time, T-Mobile told him he had to negotiate with the manager of the store he bought the phone at (Good Hope Road, Milwaukee). He called the store to make sure a manager was there -- and the staff refused to say one way or the other! He went to the store. The manager was there... but refused to leave his office to meet his customers. Instead he sent his minions out to talk for him. And he refused to solve the problem. Have escalated all this to Better Business Bureau.

We've had a Family plan with T mobile and the service was fine until around 6 months ago. Calls wouldn't go through in areas where we used to have no problems. Then my wife's phone messed up and we had to replace it.

Long story short the replacement was a lemon and I spent 2 and a half hours problem solving with customer service somewhere across the ocean. Very nice lady, she was "so sorry" a lot but couldn't really help much beyond that.  I had to go to the store so that we could revisit all the troubleshooting techniques that I had tried the night before with my new friend. Except that they we're condescending and rude at the store. 

Ended up that the wiz kids in pink t shirts couldn't fix it either. They'd have to order a replacement. Saturday the phone was available for pick up but instead of driving to the Tmobile store and picking up a replacement phone I switched the family over to Verizon. 

Guess who's got fantastic cell service and 3 new phones........

Since Sprint merged with T mobile my service has been a nightmare. I barely have one bar in my house when I have had full bars for the last 10 plus years. Calls drop constantly, my emails are spotty when delivering. I don’t know what sprint was smoking when they agreed to this

This is the worst network ever... No service, no intelligent tech support,  no internet.  I was stuck on a highway in south Mississippi with a temperature 104 and no shade for 2 hours.  Im old with diabetes. This service is history.  I am telling everyone I know.  I did call to complain the 1st tech hung up and the second laughed.

I had Sprint for nearly 20 years, rarely a dropped call. I received an email that T Mobile bought Sprint and that my current phone would no longer work on their network soon so I needed to get a new one. The Samsung Galaxy I turned in, I had it about 5 years and it looked and worked like new. I had to turn that phone in for another Samsung phone which was fine but the network is horrible! Several times a day I restart my phone trying to get a connection. When I click on something, I have to wait like it's AOL dial up! I take road trips and constantly lose my GPS signal, that isn't safe! I run a store from my phone and now it takes forever to list items, that is money lost! I have tried twice to get help from Customer Service, that was a joke, I just wasted 2 hours of my time. I can't believe in 2021 a mobile provider is this bad. My mother's phone that she bought at a dollar store has a better network than T Mobile. This is ridiculous! Definitely looking into another provider asap! ZERO stars, do not reccomend.

Since T Mobile purchases Sprint ( I was with Sprint 20 years until last Friday)
Their service went from great to bad on both of our phones, cost me a large deal due to no service in a area I always had service, on two phones on same plan, and they inspected my phones said they were fine. 
Many areas where I had service went to no service, so something went bad, their customer service was terrible as well. (my personal experience after 20 years and my personal opinion)
We changed last Friday and what a difference.
If I could leave a minus 100% I would.

If I can give a negative star I would give a -5. They're listing two of my lines as lost/stolen when I never reported them. They stated I need to pay for the stolen phones which they admitted is active on my account. Then they tried to upgrade the "stolen" lines by adding a mystery down payment. All I want is to have the error, which is Their fault removed from my line, and after 4 days, over 6hrs on the phone each day, the issue remains, while at the end of the call they just hang up leaving no notes for the next agent, so I must explain this story again, and again. Mind you, every agent is from an overseas country with ZERO courtesy. They must make a sale. The worst, worst, worst. I'm leaving this garbage company asap.
I've just had a fantastic call from customer service. The girl I spoke with was so accommodating and that it why I've been a customer for 20+ years

The service has been getting worse and worse for my husband and I, so we switched to Verizon. While at Verizon, we were told that unlocking our phones should be easy and then we would be good to go. 

We tried doing it through our phones, didn't work. My husband spent a while on hold with customer service, just to be told Samsung has to do it. Samsung said they are wrong and T-Mobile has to do it. 

T-mobile put in a request saying it can take up to 72 hours, that excludes weekends and we switched Friday. We have been without phones, it's insane that they expect people not being able to use their phones for so long. I have seen so many complaints about people waiting weeks or months. There seems to be an issue unlocking T-mobile phones bought from Samsung which they never fixed.

I was told by others that I can pay someone on ebay that can do it within a few hours, 24 at most. I looked and found out it's true and there are legit people on there. If they can do it within a few hours, it shouldn't take so long for T-mobile. Unfortunately, my phone is newer and would be over $100 to unlock.

They need a better system for unlocking phones and shouldn't excluded weekends. Stuck with phones we can't use and started and paying for a new service we can't use.

TMOBILE REPS WILL CALL U A LIAR TO UR FACE AND THEY MAKE PROMISES TO THEIR CUSTOMERS THAT THEY DO NOT FULFILL AND THEY LEAD CUSTOMERS ON AND THEY HAVE BAD SERVICE AS A WHOLE OF TMOBILE ..NO NO I WOULD NOT RECCOMMEND TMOBILE TO NO ONE ..I LOVE VERIZON NOW THAT I GOT PHONE FROM THEM AND THEY TREAT U AS FAMILY AT VERIZON

T-Mobile iPhone 12 is the worst phone I ever switch to  
T-Mobile service is really bad plus they charge you to make payments arrangements and charge you late charges they charge for everything

They can't seem to understand that John and Jack are one and the same. My military ID says John and everything else I have says Jack. They are the ONLY company who can't seem to pull two brain cells together to figure that out. 13 years with them and it is time to go somewhere else. T-Mobile is like the stupidest blonde you will ever talk too

They just stole my iPhone 8 and doesn't allow me to end the contract.

I've been a customer for 3.5 years. They are the worst possible service provider ever. I got on to a plan where I traded in my iphone8 for an iphone 12 pro 8 months ago. I was not really happy with their terrible coverage here since 3 years, I'm on a family plan and I was the one who convinced everyone to hop on to TMobile.  The reception was so bad at all times we tolerated the bad service for 3+ years and finally decided to get on to Verizon. Now TMobile starts acting up. They want me to pay for the device full amount. I was ready to pay the remaining amount after deducting the iPhone8's amount. They said I had to wait for the whole 30 months to recoup the iPhone8's price. I CANNOT put up with the crappy network for 24 months more. Now they want me to pay the full amount for the phone and they kept my iPhone8.

The sales people lied to me and my family telling we would get two new phones to switch service ro T-Mobile. We switched and got billed $850 for one phone and they would not correct their lie or error.

Ask T Mobile if they would give me a new phone for half off since I ruined my phone. after two trips to the store 30 miles away still didn't have a phone so they promised me they would give me a new one for free after dealing with them for 2 hours on the phone but the contract they sented to sign said I had to pay for it. spend another 2 hours getting that fixed. Last 8 years with t mobile has been a real pain. If you are looking for new service run from t mobile.

Inept employees at Mill Creek Washington location were rude,condescending, arrogant and unprofessional. I will never refer anybody to T-Mobile. Was a customer for 14 years. Tried to switch to Verizon and they wouldn't give me my account number or account pin number. When they finally did they gave me the wrong numbers, apparently to make it difficult for me to switch. I'm waiting on a job interview phone call and I can't even receive phone calls because of this. What if I had an emergency situation involving my children? There are so many what ifs.
I don't even Think T-mobile  Deserve one star man this  Internet service is so damn bad 😡

T Mobile ripped us off.  

We are two widows:  My mother is 101.  I am 72.  We have very little income.  

A series of accounting and billing errors as well as promises occurred and we ended up having to pay for the so called free phone (200 dollars) and for time we did not use the phone despite being told the billing would NOT start until the phone was activated. 

I pointed out the errors but they couldn't care less.  To them 266 dollars for nothing means only more money in their coffers.  

Had I known all this would occur, I would have just stayed at ATT.  At ATT, I paid the same amount for 6 1/2 years, no hassles, no problems.  They also offered excellent customer service.  I just left due to a promotiion for a good quality free phone at T Mobile.  I had no idea they would blame me for their errors and be nasty with me on the phone and charge me for a phone that was supposed to be free. 

Don't bother using T Mobile.  Find another carrier, even if it costs more!

I got t-mobile due to a deal offer online . I have had a cell phone and had many different providers over the years . Their customer service was good until I ported my number . I left them becuase f so many dropped calls and lousy reception in a lot of places . 
I had auto pay and ad my bil using it , alway son  time of course for a coupel of years . When i ported the number the payment for the upcoming cycle was nt yet due , so I of course cancelled with them and moved on . These POS charged me again after I ported the number elsewhere and refused to refund . The said I did not cancel the auto pay in a timely enough manor . What a crock of @#$% . A billion dollar company screwing me for $50 payment . What SCUM. They had more dropped calls than any other major carrier . They will find a way to rip you off for sure.
Horrible should of never merged with ducking sprint

T-Mobile sucks. Ordered 55+ from store, told salesman twice no magenta plan....wanted $55 for 2 lines. Today got billed for Magenta plan. After 5pm. Tried every way to get support...finally tried to create a login....code took so long to reach my phone it was no longer accepted. Can't send photos on this POS company's infrastructure. 5G is a lie. Sorry I was duped......NEXT DAY...tried to call support after paying bill within a few hours of receiving the text to find both phones shut off....not even able to dial 611 to reach T-mobile sucks. I live 15 miles from nearest store so had to make the drive. Agent told me I was suspended today due to non payment, and also now owe a $42 late fee!!!! He then took 20min to get it sorted out with Tmobile. Last night had the other phone on the plan send me a pic from the next room. It arrived 30min later!!! Tmobile sucks!!

Haven't been able to do ANYTHING on my phone that uses any data. Yes, I have the cheapest plan, and it shows. I've been trying to send an email with 2 pictures for 3 hours now, just to give you an example. Told they were updating to 5G in my area.....I just want to be able to text or email pics. But, this is impossible even on a 4G network. Didn't have this issue when it was Sprint....it sucks. Pay the the higher rate and be happy, this is complete BS.

I have been a Sprint customer for over five years and have had little to no issues.  The second that T-Mobile took over, it's been nothing but issues. Support is of absolute no help.

I had a fine working iphone 8 plus, but was contacted about a new promo where if you turn in your old device, you receive $400 and some odd dollars off your new phone.  Because of Covid there was no option to go in store to get this promo, it was only online.  So I purchased a new Iphone 12 pro max, which had the payment of $24/monthly with my trade in.  When they sent my phone, they sent a T mobile sim card, and this was the first of my knowledge that T mobile was taking over. I sent my old phone back and when they received it, they decided that it was only worth $55, not the $400 something.  There was no option to accept the amount, or not.  Which I find interesting since it was made to only be online, I was pretty much forced to give them my old, working phone, and keep the new one that I was now going to have to pay $46/monthly.  When I asked if there was a way to just get my old phone back, they said no and that I had no other options.  I understand it said in small print somewhere "eligible device." but the phone worked perfectly fine, and if I were in store, there's no way I would have gone through with the transaction, and they obviously knew this, which is why they made this an online only deal.  

When I received my new phone, I received a new T mobile Sim card.  Since having T mobile since March, I have never had worse service in my entire life.  Even when I have a crunched up flip phone in highschool.  If I am not connected to wifi, forget it.  Nothing will load, all my calls drop, I'm constantly missing text messages, etc. 

Every time I contact support, I am on the phone/chat with them for over an hour, and they always end up saying they're so sorry for the concern, but T mobile doesn't charge taxes! .......That's what you're working with? 

In the midst of trying to actually get service, they "updated" our plans without telling us.  So now my bill is $54 more per month because it switched over to a tmobile plan.......without asking or giving the option to get out of the contract.  I understand somewhere way down the line when I clicked a button to be able to purchase a phone service, that I agreed to this, but how damn sketchy of a company. 

So now, I'm paying $54 dollars more a month for a phone I don't get service and no one can help me figure it out.  Oh, the kicker? I can't cancel because when I bought the new phone, it put me in a new 24 month contract.  I have been trying to see what it would take to get out of the contract, but it seems no one magically knows the answer to that and I always give up after an hour and a half on the phone with someone each time. 

Today I've been on chat support while working for 1 hour and 24 minutes and all they've told me so far is that t mobile is out right now and has been for 11 days, and they don't know when it will be back up, and apologized.  I told them that I need a solution before I leave the chat today, but I'm sure I won't get one.  Well, they cancelled the chat and I had to start from scratch with a different rap, AGAIN! I've never had a poorer customer service or more of a hatred for a company before and I was forced to have century link internet for 5 years, (if you know, you know.)

Do yourself a favor and literally use solo cups and a string before switching to sprint/tmobile. What an absolute joke of a company, it's disgusting!!!!

Tmobile said I would get a refund. After waiting over a month, I never got it. 
When I called customer service, they said they had no record to show a refund amount. 

I had to go down to the local store to get a receipt showing the return and email it to the supervisor at Tmobile. I was told they would call me in a few days and let me know when it was fixed. 

They never called. 

I had to call and when I got a hold of someone on the phone, they still acted as though they didn't understand what was going on, even after I emailed the receipt and all. 

Do not do business with this company. They will rip you off and promise money back but never do. My lawyer likes this.

Fist of its has a really bad network. And second reason I had a new iPhone from t-mobile it’s was in finance after l paid full price because l will come back my country and t mobile costumes service sed me that your phone unlocked. That means l can use it in my country. 
 They only said my phone uncooked but they didn’t register. l lost my money  for international call. The costumer service tell me you need reset you phone so in fist  time l make soft reset but nothing is changed. After l had a hard reset same story. It took one week and after that they sad sorry we forgot registrate you phone. 
 The people in costume service are really stupid or they don’t care about the costumer

I have been a T-mobile customer since getting my first cell phone more than 15 years ago. I had a monthly plan for quite a while, then switched to a prepaid plan because I never used up all my minutes. This saved a lot of money because I work online and mostly communicate through email, Messenger, and Skype.

Then in 2021, my prepaid minutes started vanishing very quickly. Every time I tried to make a call, I got a message saying my account balance was too low. When I refilled my account, the balance would drop without me using the phone. I don't text or use my phone much, but my balance kept going down inexplicably. A short call to pay a bill or make an appointment was costing me about $10 each time and my account balance dropped to nearly nothing even when my phone was turned off.

So after 7 months of this and conflicting information from T-Mobile reps, I decided to transfer my phone number to a different carrier and that in itself turned into a huge ordeal. As soon as I tried to switch carriers, I was unable to log into my account with my usual ID. When I finally got in, the "account" link on my online account went to a "restricted" page and I had to waste over an hour verifying my identity and jumping through hoops to get basic info I needed about my account.

It made me wonder if T-Mobile had a way of knowing I'd removed their SIM card from my phone and was making it hard for me to change providers. That probably wasn't the case, but to have so much difficulty getting out after months of very poor service was the last straw that makes me really despise T-Mobile as a company.

DON"T GO THERE!
I have been a Sprint customer since 2003. Yes, there were some bugs during all those years. However, Sprint quickly and graciously took care of my husband's and my issues. We happily continued with them, found their coverage to be good, and always felt we were getting an excellent value for the product and service.
With the merger with T-Mobile, ALL has changed. Our phones no longer operate correctly, we are getting poor service, and voice mail on my husband's iPhone is nonexistent. We have spent multiple days and too many hours, across several months, trying to resolve all our issues due to the merger. Tech support cannot correct the issues, customer support is horrendous, promises to call my husband and I on specific dates & times. Never happened. They are unwilling to make any concessions to keep a long-time customer happy. After 18 years with Sprint, we are moving on.

When I canceled my account it didn't cancel my credit card. So they took money off of it that I needed for another bill. Called them they canceled my card. June 14 is when I canceled they told me to call back on July 14 to process a 2 or 3 business days for it to go back on to card. It's July 20 and they tell me refund was canceled. They took advantage of a disabled person with limited income.
Been with TMobile for over 10 years and they used to be decent but with recent changes their service barely works, dropped calls, non-existent wifi and 5G coverage they claim is a JOKE!!

I am so very unhappy with T Mobile. All my contacts did not switch over, I can't get help for anything, I'm stuck with a watch that doesn't hold a charge long, can't call out or receive calls, I have elderly parents, and a Grandbaby I watch, and can't rely on this carrier. I should of stayed with Verizon. T Mobile is the WORST system. I will not recommend them to anyone. I can't even do ANYTHING in my garage either, with this carrier. So very sad!

T-Mobile has the terrible service in Spokane Wa and Sun City Center Fl.  Can’t even connect to test speeds because it too slow, when I do connect its only downloads at 1-2 mbs.  Worst service I have ever had!!!!

I would rather use paper cups and sting to communicate

Spent 16 hours (keep track) trying to get a new phone for my daughter.  Wanted to free iPhone 12 as advertised.  On hold, waiting for supervisors, transferred and mysterious drop call and still nowhere.  Be with sprint for 5 years.  Time to go back to Verizon!

I left T-Mobile over 10 years ago after being lied to and stolen from and went to Sprint. Now T-Mobile Box Sprint and are back to their same old BS games their service sucks. It reminds me of cricket in Salt Lake City when they come out it is that bad. When you call and try and get it fixed they just lie to you just like before.

The connection in the new location I have moved to has terrible reception for T-Mobile. So I've been trying to switch carriers where reception is great for the locals. I bought my phone through Samsung Unlocked for T-mobile. However, when I try to activate my new SIM card I realize my phone is locked. Went back and forth between 5 different T-mobile and Samsung customer support, each giving me different information to which none has worked or just not applicable. Both blaming each other for the issue. However, considering the phone is locked through carrier I cannot possibly see how it would be Samsung's responsibility. T-mobile recently removed the option to give out the PIN to unlock the phone for whatever odd reason. In addition, continuously blamed that I did not purchase through T-mobile therefore do not have the Unlock phone app by T-mobile. I requested my phone to be unlocked, that is not an excuse by federal law. I report my issue to the FCC. So after 2 weeks there is an automatic install of a T-mobile .apk package. After which I was able to unlock my phone.

Sprint customer to T-Mobile merger. I been a customer with sprint for 18 years. Sprint coverage and data plan has always worked well for me. I enjoyed being a sprint customer. The last 6mths have been horrible with T-Mobile. I have had issue with dropped calls, coverage, no data, phone only wanting to work if I use WiFi for apps. T-Mobile  sent me a new sims card saying my coverage and data would improve. However, I started getting dropped calls, I made the switch to Verizon today.

When I signed on to the ( No Contract ) plan. I was told no hidden fees ,etc by the agent. And prepaid for services.  I then left T- Mobile to go to another carrier. A year later. I get a $ 75 bill on my credit report. Nothing ever came from T- Mobile or a letter from a collection agency. I called the collection agency. And they told me it was because I cancelled my plan and had no contract. That conversation went nowhere. After some investigation. I come to realize. I am one of to many to list with this complaint. T- Mobile has been sued. Have constant litigation and complaints . If you go to these guys. You get what you get. You've been warned.
Forced into T-Mobile from Sprint. Awful coverage. Dropped calls, no connection to call or text. Area of dropped calls and no service is growing. Extremely unhappy. Stay away!

T Mobile does not have enough towers to actually get what you’re promised. We took them at their word and switched from US Cellular looking for a better and yet cheaper plan. Is there such a thing? Probably not. You get what you pay for. Right? My husband works for Union Pacific Railroad and he didn’t get any kind of service, even in big towns. We kept getting a voice mail or lose a signal if we even got through.

We gave them our old cell phone to trade in for iPhone 12 Pro Max. Our phones were in perfect shape and worked perfectly. We wanted out of T Mobile to return to US Cellular. T Mobile has a so-called return no fault policy so you are suppose to get refunded everything you pay. However, they will keep your old phones and give you nothing for them, which screws you out of getting a good deal at another place with a trade in on your old phone. We got paid back what we paid the day we signed up. However, they gave us nothing for our older phones.  Feel they stole our phones. We gave them back our new phones. The manager said he’d check into getting something for our phone and he said he’d call me and let me know. He never called. 

Someday they may have good service with more towers, but if you’re expecting great unbelievable 5G service, you’re only fooling yourself.

90+ minutes on hold to talk to an overseas (Philippines) call center…only to be cut off when asking for a supervisor because the representative couldn’t understand my problem (because their English was that bad).

My warranty exchange phone (my 3rd one) was “cancelled” while standing IN the store, waiting for a representative. Showed up within my window to do so. T-mobile couldn’t “uncancel” it…so now have to go back while they send the replacement phone back to HQ and send out ANOTHER one, even though the store still had the replacement on hand.

Spent another almost 2 hours on the phone with “customer service,” trying to get the “cancelled phone status” reversed.

Total=4 hours. No replacement phone, no consideration from T-Mobile that they keep sending broken/refurbished phone to replace a new bought one…and still have to go through the whole process again when the replacement replacement phone comes back in.

Just got back from driving from Los Angeles to Texas…only had a signal about a third of the time…including driving the 10 Freeway most of the way…just as bad on the 15/215 to Nevada.  USELESS in Big Bear…and anywhere west of Westlake.

The sold me a new Samsung A71…now waiting for my 3rd/4th one under warranty…hasn’t worked right from day one. …first thing I was told to do to “fix” things, was to turn OFF 5G on my new 5G phone!

We have 6 lines between us on 3 different accounts…so almost $300 a month worth of phone bills. You would have thought that they would care enough about that much business that a once off “loyalty” credit for putting up with their shortcomings and being jerked around might have been offered…but not only wasn’t it offered, it was REFUSED.  I got a $5 credit for the phone they wouldn’t let me pick up…and that’s it.  

I/we moved from Verizon because of their sucky customer service…but at least I could still use my phone.  T-Mobile (US reps) will sweet talk you (if you can even manage to get one)…and give EVERY excuse under the sun why you are having issues with your service, but not do anything about it, but keep taking your money.

Seriously, T-Mobile SUCKS.  Their customer service has gotten exponentially worse since their merger with Sprint…and their signal NEVER works…even where it should!

If you use your phone for business in any way, shape or form…RUN…don’t walk…over to any of the carriers that use the Verizon network.  You might not appreciate Verizon’s poor customer service, or their more expensive plans…but you get what you pay for…at least you won’t lose business because you can’t successfully complete a call uninterrupted.

You have been warned!

HORRIBLE AND UNACCEPTABLE CUSTOMER SERVICE ! Really really disappointed . Some black lady Princess hang up on me and never called me back . WASTE OF TIME . NOBODY DONT GO TO T- Mobile . It’s a pain in the butt . People keep playing games and transferring me over and over , so far I’ve been transferred 5 times ! And I keep repeating my issue, how unprofessional it is . PEOPLE ARE RUDE ! PUTTING ME ON LONG HOLDS ! REALLY REALLY UNSATISFIED

DON'T GO WITH T-MOBILE!!!  Go with literally ANY other company, just NOT T-MOBILE!!!  It's NOT worth the free iPhone -- trust me!!!  They will cheat you when you try to move to another company, and get collection agencies after you for a bill that you never should have been charged.  Their customer service is HORRIBLE -- just read the reviews!  And I was a perfect customer for over 2 years, paying every bill on time and in full.  And at the end they try to gouge you in the most despicable way, and customer service representatives (who you can barely understand) treating you like dirt.  They don't care -- they are thousands of miles away, why would they care?  Just trust me, anyone who is trying to find out if T-Mobile is worth the free iPhone --IT REALLY IS NOT.  THEY ARE AWFUL!!!

Called T-Mobile two days ago informing the rep that all 4 of our phones were hacked & hijacked. The agent confirmed it was a serious situation. We then Informed the agent that my mother and myself have received multiple death threats that day and the days prior. The Agent confirmed that the hackers could access the cam, mics, messages, location and anything we do on our mobile devices. She said a virus such as this could not be removed and we would need new devices and those could be picked up at the nearest Mobile Store and we would need to go there when they opened. She was Supposed to call back on the ****# or the ****# if we were disconnected. Almost 2 hrs into the conversation the call was dropped and 3 days later we still have not received a call from T-Mobile. Went to store today to get new device's but the phone's were not sent by T-mobile. We have had no way to communicate with anyone including authorities for three days and the old devices are all we have, but using them puts us at risk because the hackers can locate us, and they know everything we do and say on those devices. They know if and when we leave our homes and my house has been broken into 5 times after I leave and once with me at home. Went to the store to purchase a phone, did not care what phone, just any phone that was in stock, don't care which one, just something so we would have a way to communicate with anyone or someone in confidentiality, or a way to call police... Just need a damn phone.... We are not allowed to sign into our Gmail or Google accounts to get our contacts so we do not have any phone numbers for friends or relatives. So the rep at the store kept wanting us to "Upgrade your device"... Don't want to upgrade. We are trying to stay alive.... "You'll have to upgrade sir".... Lady we are under attack. We just want a phone, preferably a flip phone.... "You will have to upgrade".... Goddamn what part of this is life or death you didn't hear??? She says she needs to get her computer from the back, 10 minutes goes by and she returns. She was explained every word of our situation. Which makes the THIRD T-Mobile employee to be informed, EXPRESSING TO THEM "this is a live or death situation." I asked if we could print off one contact list at the store with their printer by using their wifi on my device and she said "absolutely not, go to the library" FOUR FU©KING TIMES!! Have been paying you @zzholes $150 a month for years, in a life or death situation, you DO NOT send phones, DO NOT call back, WILL NOT allow us to print a contact list AND WE ARE STANDING IN YOUR STORE ATTEMPTING TO SPEND ANOTHER $300 TO PURCHASE 2 PHONES THAT YOU @zzHOLES DIDNT SEND THREE DAYS AGO AND YOU WONT LET US PRINT ONE PIECE OF PAPER OFF ON YOUR PRINTER THAT IS LESS THAN 10' AWAY so we could contact friends and relatives that we haven't seen or spoken to in more than 3 days. Fu©k TMOBILE and Fu©k that fat ass b!t©h working in the Anniston store

I went to the store in April to buy iPhone SE. I was persuaded by T-Mobile's employee to get this promotion where they would add a FREE line and I would get iPhone 11 half off as a part of the promotion, which would end up being cheaper then iPhone SE. Every month since then I was being charged either for the FREE line or full amount for the monthly payment for the phone. I called every time I would get the bill and every time my payment was processed. I lost 7 hours of my life and their rep would tell me (every time I called) that this issue was fixed. Except yesterday, I was told this CANNOT be fixed, as I already have a promotion on my account from 6 years ago. I even offered to return my phone and get iPhone SE, as I didn't plan to spend that much on the phone or to be paying $40 for additional line that I don't need. I was offered an apology and that was it. The representative told me nothing can be done. That their employee made a mistake, and I am not eligible for this promotion.

I was forced to cancel T-mobile because their service did not work in my new home. Upon cancellation I was not advised that I was 2 days past my bill cycle start date and would be charged an entire month for only using 2 days of service.  Upon calling to request a refund, I spoke with a rep, and then a supervisor who essentially told my I was SOL.  Upon request to escalate further I was informed that they were the highest part of customer support and no further escalation could be made and pushed me off to fax their home office for further help.  this was after being on hold for 25 minutes on the phone with them for 50 minutes.  I was a loyal customer for over 9 years, never missed payment and never raised fuss. I am beyond furious.

I was forced to switch from Sprint to T-Mobile, which led to doubling my bill.  I decided to go through with it anyway rather than going back to At&T.  While I was at it, I upgraded our phones (4 of them) and bought new covers, etc.  The grand total was $2500 plus a two year service commitment.  Usually when I spend $5000, I expect to get something good, however, my introduction to T Mobile has been horrendous.  The first store I went to was unmanned even though they had supposedly been open for almost an hour.  The second store had very helpful employees, but it took 3-1/2 hours (YES 3-1/2 HOURS) for them to switch our service and give us four new phones.  We were given temporary phone numbers and told the new phones would "figure out" to switch.  It never happened, but I got eventually an error message and was told to login online.  Obviously my old Sprint login doesn't work but I wasn't given a new T Mobile account number at the store.  So I've been on hold an hour, on my third rep, to see if anyone can tell me my account number, all the while having to listen to their advertising propaganda about how much they love their customers.  RUN!!!!

T mobile is the worst phone and tv service I've ever had. There tv service would tell you, you left the area and it would remove over half your channels. They didn't know how to fix it. They have now cancelled their service because it didn't work.

Their phone service is the same. Their wait times are 2 to 3 hours. They will tell you that they aren't have issue with their network. But with those wait times.

It has been around 9 months. They still can't fix their service.  Your phone will tell you that you are not registerstered on the network. You get any calls ringing thru, no text messages, nothing.  You won't know it until someone comes to tell you your phone doesn't work or you try to make a call and nothing happens.
Now I have had a total of 3 phone to try to fix this problem and a sim card change. Still with the same Issue's.  They will take money off your bill but what good is that if you can depend on your phone.

They promise you things on the phone, like the store will replace your phone. Only to drive there and be told that they can't do anything for you. They will promise you 1 day delivery and when you don't get it they tell you they don't do 1 day deliveries.

They out right lie to you.
The store near me the manager is a piece of crap.
told me he could wave the charge but I'm not going to do it. I had to call the corporate office to take care of it.

After waiting 40+ minutes and sometimes 3 hours while talking with them your call will be dropped even tho you haven't moved.

STAY FAR AWAY FROM THIS COMPANY
If you don't just remember I'm now laughing at you sucker.

Spouse died and account was in her name.
Getting this fixed with this company was a nightmare.  The only bright light in the process was jane@tmobile in the Yulee office. She is a superstar and without her I could not have got it fixed. She gets 5 stars. Company gets a 1.

Been with this carrier for several years without problem. Problem had to come sometime, for me it's all about how it's handled when it does. Here's what I experienced: technicians are not sufficiently trained, long hold times to speak with same technicians. Never did get issue resolved. Horrible attitude from retail staff (went to pick up new phone which I left without). Long story short: changing carriers.

Great customer service and very poor communication. Cancelled service to go to another wireless carrier because the signal is so bad with Tmobile and what they don't tell you is that if you port out your number you get charged another full month of service you won't be using. This is the worst wireless service and I would not recommend them to anyone.On top of that they couldn't get my bill straight for the first four months with them. STAY AWAY.
Khaniya was awesome thank u hun

I have had Verizon for over 20 years and it's quite pricey. So with all these T Mobile commercials we were going to make the switch. We wanted to keep our phones and phone number. They required a card on file but I was advised I would not be charged anything until we switched our phones and started with them. They sent me SIMcards which we received 3 days after my conversationwith them. In the meantime I still hadn't received any confirmation from them regarding the monthly charge, and plan info which was to include unlimited data, talk and text in writing. I advised them that until I got a confirmation of monthly charge, etc... I will not switch phones. (Keep in mind, we are now 2 weeks in). Eventually after receiving emails in return with double talk and still no confirmation I asked them to cancel the switch and if we decide to go with T-Mobile we will go to a kiosk.
Well... I happen to have had a small pile of mail from about a weeks ago that I hadn't had the opportunity to look at because it was picked up by my teenager and put in the wrong place. In that pile was a notice from T-Mobile that stated that in order for them to continue service they would require a deposit. Basically, had I did what they wanted and switched all of our phones over, they would have then cut the service until they got this deposit. And they still never gave me monthly charge info in writing. After laughing and being very happy that I never made that change, I decided to write this review because my guess is they probably do this to people that may end up with no service or much higher monthly charges then they anticipated.
We are still probably going to change carriers eventually but definitely not T mobile.

I have a T-mobile phone and need them to activate it so I can use it to make phone calls. For various reasons, I can only do this through their service desk. I am now entering my second hour listing to terrible and distorted music while they say help will be with me "shortly." Not clear whether that means in a few hours or a few days.
I've been a customer for over twenty years. My complaint is they are constantly shoving the homosexual lifestyle down our throat in all there ads. While hoping to gain customers .
I also been a customer for 12 years with Sprint and I changed the SIM card to T-mobile  FOR BETER SERVICE ……… WORSE SERVICE EVER…. SRINT WHAT WERE YOU THINKING😡

Ever since the Sprint / T-Mobile merger there has been problems. First there were service connection issues, so their fix was to update to the T-mobile SIM. However, now that we have done that our service is worse, now we can't even get a reliable signal at home. Worst merger ever!

After 10 years with T-mobile decided to switch carrier...
They  can make as many as they want happy and encouraging commercials and statements,  but the truth is - T-mobile has no reliable service today. 
I'm driving around United States every week, and can say service and connection is bad, really bad((
Went to tmobile on church st in conway to transfer all my info to a diff. Phone and frankie was very helpful.....and I give him a 5 star for that, thanks....

This is the WORST SERVICE EVER!! I pay twice as much for 1phone that I NEVER HAVE SERVICE ON! Customer service is a joke!! I have never had a cell phone or paid for a cell phone plan which I could not use!! WORTHLESS! Don’t wast your time on customer service all they want to do is sell extr services or tell you that you need a new ph!! I just bought this phone!! I have a unlimited plan that is a JOKE..

Absolute garbage. coverage is non existant. they just emerged with sprint and now the only time I pick up my phone is to clear the constant notifications they keep sending. day and night constand notifications. when I finally got ahold of customer service some lady on speaker phone in the world's best room for acoustics and a amateur daycare service I'm assuming wasnt abale to help me. Thanks t mobile, you're in breach of your contract we didnt sign with you. Have a good one...
Worst website and customer service. Takes up to a few hours to get a hold of a person

text: TMobile customer service have done it again. They're a rip off. Cricked and bunch of liars. Don't let them have access to your checking account because they will steal and rip you off. Stay away from the crocks..

My now ex-boyfriend led me to transfer my phone data and phone number from Metro PCS to a T-Mobile phone. We did the transaction inside a T mobile business, He instructed the personnel to place his own personal SMS card inside the phone. That resulted in a different location programming on the phone. That resulted in a different location programming on my phone. Therefore a small confusion occurred when I needed 911 emergency services. Also while inside the T mobile business, he signed a 2-year contract using a taped-up Fedex ID card. He used his work ID to sign under a different last name. Pure fraud. Therefore the name on the 2-year contract differed from the name on his license. 
I contacted T mobile service 11 Jul 2021 to understand my options. The customer service did not follow standard operating procedures of apologizing for the inconvenience. My options are to change my phone number and still remain on the fraud account. Therefore responsible for any charges added to the fraud account. Or create a business account by bringing company documentation into a T mobile service building. The reality is I do not own a business. Therefore T mobile can not do anything to counter fraud. Therefore T mobile services are supporters of fraud, stalking, identity theft, and human trafficking. May my post help others to survive in the state of Georgia.

First, we have been with T-Mobile from 1-6-2021 from leaving AT&T after at least 25yrs or more. WE read some review about other wireless company T-Mobile was not the best, but on the recommendation of my nephew who had T-Mobile. We went to the local T-M store ask a lot of question get all the right answer and get 1 new phones free and 1 at a half price with a lower service price, after all those yrs with AT&T we couldn't get any type of deal on new phones. Now so far we have had zero problem with T-M. One reviewer said they had bad reception in the Tampa Bay area I will BE traveling to St. Pete soon I will let u know. Now last part of this review happen 7-7 this yr this week.  I call T-M for a up grade of are plan to ad netflix to my account, the Customer Service rep. name Andre is the definition of  the words (CUSTOMER SERVICE) look it up. He was on the phone with Netflix and his supervisor trying to get use service for about 2 hours (LOOK IT UP THE TIME IT ALL TRUE) he kept saying I will call you back Hang with me I get it done, at the end of all that, HE did it. I put down 4 stars, if I could put a half star ANDRE would get 1 an a half to make 5+ Thank you Sir.
Can I give 0 stars the customer service in Philippines I have been on the phone for 2 hours and still counting ...I will be transferring my account very soon!!!!!!!!!

I bought my plan and phone online T-Mobile site. I received it with no clear instructions on how to transfer my phone number since I decided to keep my old one. I went through the process at checkout and thought that was enough. I have had to research how to transfer data and even to find a phone number to speak to a tech assist for T-Mobile. This has become more of a chore than I signed up for. Not a smooth process and frustrating. Not to mention I was less than impressed with the Galaxy S21. What a piece of garbage. Lacks simple things that I miss like not being able to plug in ear phones while charging. Not having a finger print for biometrics. Instead it comes chock full of spaces to place apps. Slide here slide there....Why do I need so many options as to where to place my apps!? Oh and be prepared to type in your password for every single app you open because the phone wont remember it like it claims to. This phone is nonsense.

so my mother and I walk into a t-mobile store because her phone stopped charging after only 3 years, we came in at first for a replacement for the phone. because she has important things for work on the phone, we didn't have time to send out the phone for a replacement. they didn't have a replacement phone for her so instead we asked if there were any deals. usually, there are deals like buy one get one free, which would be good if you could use it. so after about 90 minutes we finally get served and we ask if there are any buy one get one free deals. the only worker there was still working with another customer so the manager wanted to help us over the phone, now if this was the manager or not I do not know. the "manager" said that there was a buy one get one free deal on the Samsung galaxy s21. and all we would have to pay would be one phone+tax and the tax for the other phone. We said that is ok. the manager said to give back the phone to the worker and that he would tell the worker what to do. now we are 2 hours into this so we are rushing so we can get home. the worker gave us the phone and switched the sim cards from the phones we had and gave it back. he never gave my phone sim card holder back from my old phone. So once we are home we look at the bill and found out we got charged for both phones and an upgrade fee. we then realize that the worker gave us the Samsung Galaxy s21+ which didn't have the deal and was 200$ more than the s21. So we call up customer support and they said that we will still have the 800$ credit (how much the s21 is). after a bit of thinking we just decide to keep the phones. the next day we get a phone call saying that we didn't get the 800$ credit and that we would have to pay for both phones. We also then found out that they added 2 new lines without telling us. Even though this happened we will still use t-mobile. if you don't have to or want to upgrade your phones then the monthly price to have unlimited data, talk, and text is very hard to beat.

I could (and probably should) write an in depth article about how T-Mobile completely misrepresents the truth of promises made by them. However, they are NOT to be trusted. We switched from (expensive) AT&T to T-Mobile because of the advertised rates and coverage. What a terrible mistake. We live in the Tampa, FL area and T-Mobiles coverage is terrible. From dropped calls to terrible data connectivity, they rank at the bottom of the wireless carriers. 

They lied about the rate and refused to correct it. They refused to provide me with the chat log from the signup conversation with their representative Steven, which would clearly define the promises made. I will never have an online chat with any company without recording it to a file (my mistake for not doing so with the T-Mobile representative).  

We had multiple telephone conversations during our relationship about the terrible service, the promised rate not being honored, etc and most of them lasted 45-60 minutes or more because of getting transferred to off-shore support (who barely speaks English). They constantly apologized for our experiences, but nothing ever changed. 

I could go on and on, but I’m not wasting my time. We switched to Verizon and have had zero issues. Verizon has delivered everything promised and we have fantastic coverage. We have one person to contact if we have issues and don’t have to spend hours on hold with them. 

I highly recommend avoiding T-Mobile.

Well, I am coming to SHINE some SUNLIGHT about my T-Mobile!  I’ve had an OVERALL GREAT experience with T-MOBILE. Like with ANY company, they have their kinks to work out, but MY experience has been, pretty much, GREAT. Let me start by saying I HIGHLY RECOMMEND the CHAT customer service versus calling in. The CHAT customer service is 100% satisfactory with me! They haven’t missed a beat(5-0) with whatever I need! Granted my issues aren’t MAJOR, but they are friendly, fast, professional, and willing to go the extra mile without being promoted. My daughter has Verizon wireless and my service picks up where hers doesn’t and vice versa(at times). I enjoy my T-Mobile.

T-mobile is a terrible cell phone service and company to deal with. The billing practices are meant to hide fees and their own employees can not understand the billing. Don't dare decide to upgrade and shop online because installments on a "device" and leasing are two very different meanings. I upgraded to a phone that showing it was $6 more than what I was paying and that has turned out to be a lie. My bill is $40 more and all Tmobile can tell me basically is I was tricked into a contact to higher my bill. My so called upgraded phone is not any different or any better than previous phone. Service is spotting and unstable. Tmobile is a rip off!!! I left once than got trapped back with them because Tmobile is now a monopoly cell phone service.

I had a great impression of T-Mobile for the first month. Getting their mobile internet sent to my home and installed was seamless. The internet functioned fine for internet browsing but that was about the extent of it. I require decent internet for videoconferencing at work, among other things. When it came time to cancel, the process was absolutely abysmal; I spent 3 hours of my Friday night waiting on hold and going person to person (ultimately with 3 people), with each one trying harder than the last to try to get me to stay with the service. They just would not take no for an answer. Big picture, I was hoping that in years to come T-Mobile's service would be sufficient for my needs - in which case I would give them another shot. The cancellation process was so painful that I will not ever seek out any of their services again.

I have been with Sprint/T-Mobile for 6 months and have had nothing but issues. I have been into 2 different stores 4 times, called customer service multiple times, still terrible service!! I have spent HOURS on the phone with them and they only offer apologies, no solutions. We have five lines and every person has issues with the service. At our lake house, I have to go outside and walk down the street to get service and Customer services said this area was in their 4G area! My bill increases by $15 every month! Seriously, I would rather have a land line and no cell phone than have to deal with Sprint/T-Mobile ever again!!

Absolutely the worst now. Been with T mobile since they were Ariel. Upgraded to the 5g now two tin cans and a string would work better.
Customer service is horrible and they will lie to you to get you hooked into the worst carrier out there.
Was told twice that the upgrade includes phones and more coverage. And that is pure BS.
Since the upgrade we get 80% more dropped calls and less area.

I would truly rate them less than one star if possible.

Haven’t been able to get cell service on my phone since July 1st (and poorly previously for at least a year), could basically only use it when on WiFi. Now, not at all. When I try to open an app or make a call the LTE that claims it was connected just drops off and I have to hope within a minute or two it’ll connect, or just give up. 90+% of the time I just give up. What’s the point of carrying a phone?! This was in LA, Vegas, and the road in between so clearly very populated areas that should have no issues like this. 
Customer service was immediately talking over me when trying to tell him what the timelines and issues had been (this is the 3rd call to them in the span of 3 months). Finally listened, said he was troubleshooting- and after an hour, now my phone is going even more berserk. Even on WiFi it drops the outgoing calls after 10 seconds, if it hadn’t just been a failed call already, and then the phone starts to shortcircuit. It’s an old phone, iPhone 7, but in perfect condition and no issues aside from this. 

When I couldn’t even call back to customer service, I messaged in on the website to try to get help, and the rep Jayne offered me the option of calling a different customer service number. I asked her how I should call when my phone doesn’t work. Her response? Find another phone. SUPER HELPFUL. 
0 stars from start to finish- switching off of this awful carrier today.

One star because I can’t give them less. Customer service is horrible. Wait over half hour to still be not served properly. Put on hold multiple times and disconnect. Every time you are transferred you must give the imie number for each phone.
Absolute trash, signal is hot garbage sitting on top of a cell tower...
This may be the worst service provider... they acquired my service carrier and now it is garbage. Verizon is over-priced... going to Try AT&T. Sick of this s#$t...

Don't go with TMobile! I ordered a hotspot and hotspot plan through them over the phone. That was the easy part. At the time I went online to my TMobile account to cancel my plan, it would't let me without contacting customer service. I called customer service to cancel my plan and they said I need a pin number in order to cancel. There is no where on my online account to set up a pin number and they won't let you set one up over the phone. They said I had to go into a Tmobile Store and set up a pin number in order to cancel my plan. The closest store is 2 hours away from me. They are quick to take your money over the phone but make it very difficult and inconvenient to cancel your plan. I don't recommend TMobile.

By far the WORST cell coverage I have ever had.  I recently switched from Verizon after seeing the 5G ad from T-Mobile. TERRIBLE DECISION. I live in Fredericksburg VA and I work in Washington DC.  Both places I consistently drop calls, can not receive or send texts. Absolute trash. I reached out to customer service about the service issue. But unfortunately you can't fix trash service from a trash company.  If you are debating switching to this company.  DO NOT.  The 5G is a lie. Check the BBB as well.  Do not waist your money or time.

I ordered the iPhone 12 pro two days ago and the rep that was servicing service sucked as far as understanding what i needed to be done at the present time. employee number: zu504664. she placed my order and gave me the wrong charger, she gave me a car charger ( i have no car). this was 39.99, now i have to call and get a refund which is very very time consuming and super frustrating. so i get this rep : Jennard on the phone telling me to order a new charger and pay for shipping fee and then he can proceed with my refund. COMPLETELY  unless and waste of time. A bunch transfers to different departments and too many language barriers. I get transferred to another sales rep name Danielle employee id # gd753995 and she leaves me on hold and gives me nothing but attitude. This customer service for this device was very frustrating and i almost felt like cancelling my service with this company.

Somehow they got my cell phone number as I did not sign up for anything nor did I request any information!!! Also they have no email - you have to call!!  Totally unacceptable!!!!!!!  Will NEVER use T-Mobile due to this experience.
Bad customer service my bill is at 715 and they still won't help me. I been a customer for over 10 years and they still pushed me a side. Please be careful with this phone company.

Service is terrible! I have been on hold for over an hour yesterday waiting for customer service to answer, so I hung up and I am still waiting today for over an hour! Forget about getting any kind of refund that is due you. I cancelled a month ago instead of my refund they confirmed I am suppose to get I got another bill. When you call the store where I canceled the service, they always say we cannot no longer help you, that you have to contact customer service. I WILL NEVER recommend their service to anyone!

T-Mobile is full of it. New phone and two boosters later and still can't get any bars. I had nothing but problems with them. We saw a lady talking on her phone at the springs and I asked her what she had as my girlfriend nor I didn't have service. She told us At&t that she was with T mobile and they were not that great. So after 17 years with T mobile we may just switch as well.

7/6/21 I visited T-Mobile in Moses Lake ,Wa. I was greeted graciously by Devin and given full attention and excellent costumer service. I am new in Moses Lake and appreciated the confidence you gave me that I could return for any future needs or purchases. Good representation of T-Mobile. You deserve a raise. 😊😎you are a rock star!

T-Mobile is horrible here in Dublin. I have been a T-mobile customer for 12 years and it was fine till about a year. Worst cell phone service EVERRRRR! Switched from ATT as part of one of there promos. I was LIED to when I was switching over, quite literally bait and switch. The promise of the fastest 5G network is complete and utter BS! My service is so slow. I cant even load anything that needs to connect to the internet. Calls are constantly dropped, and it is like having a 56K dial up connection but on a cell phone. 

Switching has been the worst decision ever. Plus customer service is pretty useless and a bunch of scam artists. Shame on you tmobile!
T-Mobile SUCK!!!!!! Do not do business with them. This is the worst phone service.....

Terrible.  Switched from Verizon because the rates were better.  No wonder.  Dropped calls, terrible coverage, links or photos people send won't load and using apps or the internet on your phone?  Forget it.

I would like to express some concerns as a Sprint customer regarding merging to TMobile. On July 4th, 2021 we went to a TMobile store to purchase a Hotspot device which was a current special and everything was going good until the sales associate said the computer would not allow him to activate the hotspot. The associate stated current Sprint legacy customers are blocked from making purchases and opening accounts on the TMobile network. Now we are a little confused cause we are supposed to now be part of TMobile (so we thought).


      Thinking the associate didn't know what he was doing, we drive home and call TMobile and we're advised exactly the same with the exception that if I provide a different social they will open an account under my name using someone else's social. Im sure this is probably illegal. This now seems like TMobile is discriminating against Sprint customers by denying us service for whatever reason.
